metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the generated email includes all required key facts accurately and naturally.

rubric.description: The email ignores the required facts or contradicts them.
Only a tiny fragment of one required fact appears.
Some facts are present, but multiple important facts are missing or distorted.
Most facts are present, but one important fact is missing, weakened, or ambiguous.
Almost all required facts are present, with only a minor omission or awkward compression.
Every required fact is included accurately and naturally, with no unsupported factual claims.

metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the generated email matches the requested tone in context.

rubric.description: The tone is absent, opposite of the requested tone, or unusable for the context.
The tone is very far from the requested tone.
The tone only loosely resembles the requested tone and is noticeably mismatched.
The tone is partly aligned but generic, uneven, or inconsistent.
The tone is mostly aligned with only small mismatches.
The tone closely and consistently matches the requested tone throughout.

metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the output works as a professional email.

rubric.description: The output is not usable as an email.
It barely resembles a professional email or collapses greeting, body, closing, and signature into a flat single paragraph.
Some email elements are present, but structure is incomplete, hard to scan, or mostly unformatted.
Basic email content is present, but paragraphing, greeting, body, or sign-off formatting is noticeably weak.
The email is clear and professional with only minor structure or formatting rough edges.
The subject and body are polished, clear, concise, and formatted with distinct greeting, body paragraphing, and sign-off.

metrics.reason: The email includes all required facts accurately: it specifies the May 17 meeting at 11:00 A.M., names the attendees Kenneth Lay, Marty Sunde, and Bob Williams, and asks where they should wait or go when they arrive. It also matches the intended request in a concise, polite, professional way.

The email is concise, polite, and professional throughout. It clearly asks for the exact waiting instructions for the named attendees for the May 17 meeting and uses a courteous closing, matching the requested tone very closely.

The email includes a clear subject line, a professional greeting, a concise body in a separate paragraph, and a proper closing/signature. It is well formatted and easy to scan, matching the highest structure level.

All required facts are included accurately: the meeting is for next week, the three proposed time slots are listed, recipients are asked to reply with their availability, and it says the schedule should be finalized as soon as possible.

The email is professional, concise, and collaborative: it politely requests availability, clearly lists the proposed time slots, and focuses on finalizing the schedule soon without unnecessary detail.

The email includes a clear subject line, a separate greeting (“Hi Project Team,”), a well-structured body with a short intro and bulleted time slots, and a distinct sign-off with the sender’s name and title. It is polished, concise, and easy to scan.

The email includes all required facts accurately: it identifies invoice 48217, states it was due on April 30, 2026, gives the outstanding amount as $3,450, and asks for an update on the expected payment date. It also matches the requested tone and sender/recipient context.

The email is polite and professional throughout, and it clearly follows up on the overdue invoice with the requested details and a direct request for the expected payment date. It is slightly softer than a strongly firm tone, but overall it closely matches the intended tone.

The email includes a clear subject line, a professional greeting, concise body text that is easy to scan, and a proper sign-off with sender name and title/company. The structure is polished and matches the requested professional format.

The email includes every required fact accurately: it states the original Tuesday 2:00 PM interview, explains there is a conflict on the sender’s side, offers Wednesday 9:00 AM and Thursday 3:00 PM as alternatives, identifies the Software Engineer position at BrightPath Software Solutions, notes the 90-minute duration, and asks the candidate to confirm which time slot works best.

The email closely and consistently matches the requested tone: it is professional, courteous, apologetic, and concise. It politely explains the conflict, apologizes, provides the rescheduling options, and asks for confirmation without unnecessary detail.

The email is fully polished and professionally formatted with a clear subject, distinct greeting, concise body paragraphs, and a proper sign-off. It includes all key details: the original Tuesday 2:00 PM interview, the conflict, the 90-minute duration, the alternative times Wednesday 9:00 AM or Thursday 3:00 PM, and a request to confirm availability.

The email includes the organization, early-bird deadline of September 10, 2025, conference dates of October 22 to 24, 2025, the $300 early-bird fee, the $400 regular fee, and an encouragement to register soon for the lower rate. The only missing key fact is the current date of September 5, 2025.
The email is informative and professional throughout, clearly states the key deadlines and prices, and uses a polite, encouraging reminder tone that fits the requested upbeat-professional style.
The email includes a clear subject, a distinct greeting, well-separated body paragraphs, and a professional sign-off. The structure is polished and easy to scan, matching the rubric’s top level.

The email includes all required facts accurately: it states the design review is expected to finish by Friday, 19 June, 2026, explains the delay was caused by missing stakeholder feedback, and says there is no change needed to the final launch date yet.
The email is transparent about the delay and cause, stays calm in its wording, and is reassuring by stating there is no change to the final launch date and that updates will continue.

The email includes a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, well-separated body paragraphs, and a professional sign-off. The structure is polished and easy to scan, with all key email components present.

The email includes all required facts: it states the meeting was about Q3 roadmap priorities, says the attached notes summarize the key decisions and action items, and asks recipients to send corrections by end of day Thursday. It also matches the sender and affiliation, and is clear and professional.

The email is clear, organized, and professional throughout. It states the attached meeting notes, summarizes the purpose, and politely requests corrections by end of day Thursday, matching the requested tone closely and consistently.
The email includes a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, well-separated body paragraphs, and a proper sign-off with sender name and title. It is polished, concise, and professionally formatted.

The email includes all required facts accurately: the cleanup is scheduled for Saturday from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM, volunteers are told to bring gloves and closed-toe shoes, and lunch and water will be provided. It also matches the intended invitation and includes the Riverside Neighborhood Association and Community Outreach Organizer.

The email is consistently friendly, encouraging, and appreciative: it uses warm invitations like 'We’d love to invite you,' encourages participation by saying 'Your help would mean a lot,' and closes with sincere thanks such as 'We truly appreciate your time and effort.'

The email includes a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, well-separated body paragraphs, and a proper sign-off with signature. The structure is polished and professional, and it presents the event details, requested items, and appreciation in a concise, easy-to-scan format.

The email includes all required facts: visitors must check in at the front desk, a government-issued photo ID is required for entry, and the policy begins on Monday. It is formal and informative, with no contradictions.

The email is formal and informative throughout: it uses polite business language such as "Please be advised," "In addition," and "Thank you for your cooperation," and clearly communicates the policy update, effective Monday, the front desk check-in requirement, and the government-issued photo ID requirement.
The email includes a clear subject, a distinct greeting, two well-separated body paragraphs, and a proper sign-off with name placeholder. It is formal, easy to scan, and professionally formatted.

The email includes all required facts accurately: maintenance is scheduled on Sunday from 1:00 AM to 3:00 AM, the portal may be unavailable during that window, and service is expected to resume immediately after maintenance. The tone is brief, reassuring, and professional.

The email is brief, professional, and reassuring throughout. It clearly states the maintenance window, notes the portal may be unavailable, and calmly reassures users that service will resume immediately after maintenance, matching the requested tone closely.

The email includes a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, two well-separated body paragraphs covering the outage window and expected recovery, and a proper sign-off. The structure is polished, easy to scan, and professional.
